evidenceTypesSupplied: Local derived visible-trait evidence for HERC2/OCA2, MC1R, and EDAR.
Preserved Sequencing.com Inherited Traits Genetic Test sample PDF rows used as observed output-format examples.
Consumer education references for visible traits, source binding, and direct-to-consumer trait-test limitations.
sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page parity is still pending.
No calibrated inherited-trait or child-prediction model is supplied with this fixture.

The sample report includes nutrition, blood, drug-response, immune, fitness, miscellaneous, biological-age, vitamin, infection-risk, and medication-response sections, but those sample values are not treated as local findings.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived HERC2/OCA2 rs12913832 AG call is available for inherited eye-color context. This does not determine exact eye color and cannot predict a child's eye color.
resultRows.description: The sample report includes eye-color and iris rows, while MedlinePlus frames eye color as a multi-gene trait.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: OCA2 and HERC2 are important in eye-color genetics, but eye color is influenced by multiple genes and inheritance is not a simple one-marker result.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MC1R rs1805007 CT call is available for inherited hair-color and freckle context. This is not a diagnosis, dermatology result, skin-cancer risk result, or exact appearance prediction.
resultRows.description: The sample report links MC1R to red-hair and freckle pigmentation context, while local interpretation stays limited to supplied evidence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: MC1R is a well-studied pigmentation gene, but many genes and non-genetic factors contribute to hair color, freckles, and related visible traits.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived EDAR hair-texture/thickness context row is available. This is educational context only and does not predict exact hair texture or a child's trait outcome.
resultRows.description: The sample report has hair texture and thickness rows, but exact inherited-trait outcomes require more than one fixture row.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair texture and thickness have genetic contributions, but the final trait can reflect multiple genes, development, and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.item: Inherited Traits Genetic Test Report sections and columns
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Physical Appearance, Nutrition Related, Blood Related, Drug Response, Immune System, Fitness Related, Miscellaneous Traits, Common Traits, Nutrition Suggestions, Fitness Potential, and Drug Response Summary
resultRows.description: The sample report uses Trait, Your Result, and Description columns for section tables, then separate summary panels for common traits, nutrition suggestions, fitness potential, and drug response.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the preserved Inherited Traits Genetic Test Report sample PDF; this row describes observed output structure only.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Eye color Blue vs. Brown: Brown; Blue vs. Green: Brown; Iris Crypts: Typical; Iris Furrows: Typical; Iris Rings: More
resultRows.description: The sample report includes an eye-color model note that OCA2 and HERC2 explain a large share of European eye-color variation, while eye appearance remains affected by many factors.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the Physical Appearance section and eye-color narrative; sample phenotype rows are not local eye-color predictions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Hair color Black vs. Not Black: Dark; Blond vs. Not Blond: Brown or Dark; Red vs. Not Red: Red Carrier; Curly vs. Straighter: Intermediate; Male Pattern Baldness: Typical; Hair Thinner vs. Thicker: Medium; Freckles: Typical; Skin Lighter vs. Darker: Intermediate; Skin Pigmentation: Mixed; Sun Sensitivity: Moderate; Body Mass Index: Typical
resultRows.description: The sample report groups hair, skin, and BMI-style appearance rows under Physical Appearance.
genes: Not shown for other sample-table rows

unavailableTraits: Child phenotype prediction, family inheritance certainty, exact appearance, rare-trait prediction, dermatology outcomes, and medical-risk sections are unavailable and not inferred.
Sample nutrition, blood, immune, fitness, vitamin, biological-age, and medication-response rows are preserved as output structure only.
Eye-color, hair-color/freckle, and hair-texture rows are limited to educational inherited-trait context, not identity, ancestry, diagnosis, or medical-risk interpretation.
plainEnglishLimits: Visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.
Direct-to-consumer trait tests provide estimates or context, not certainty about exact appearance.
Preserved sample rows show observed report format and source binding; they are not local personal findings unless the local run supplies equivalent validated evidence.

This report must not be used to identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, guide skincare, report disease risk, change medication, diagnose vitamin status, or make treatment decisions.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample report names OCA2 and HERC2 in the eye-color model narrative.

genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; keep separate from local MC1R evidence and avoid skin-cancer or dermatology interpretation.

genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; not a local taste result without matching local evidence.

genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local blood type or transfusion compatibility.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page sections beyond the preserved sample PDF have not been extracted.
No calibrated inherited-trait probability model or child-prediction model is supplied.

Family phenotype information, parental genotype context, rare-trait evidence, dermatology outcomes, medical-risk context, clinical pharmacogenomics context, and laboratory biomarkers require separate validated evidence and are not inferred.

Many known and unknown contributors to eye color, hair color, freckles, hair texture, nutrition traits, blood traits, immune traits, fitness traits, and medication response are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Visible traits can be influenced by multiple genes, development, age, environment, and other non-genetic factors.
The significance of individual visible-trait markers may vary across populations and should not be treated as a deterministic appearance or child-phenotype prediction.
The preserved sample report demonstrates output shape and example values; it does not provide calibrated local probabilities.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, give skincare guidance, diagnose skin conditions, report melanoma or other skin-cancer risk, diagnose vitamin status, report infection risk, or guide medication decisions.
The preserved sample PDF demonstrates output shape and example rows, not this user's exact inherited traits, blood status, immune status, fitness potential, biological age, or drug response.
